calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Oct-01-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Mia S. Blackler, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Pacific Service Credit Union.
No appearance by defendant Josiah Larkin.
Default was entered against defendant on July 8, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on September 18, 2009.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was submitted to and signed by the Court.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff pacific Servcie credit Union, and against the Defaulted Defendant Josiah Larkin as follows:

Damages/Principal - $29,198.48.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,073.72.
Attorney's Fees - $4,755.25.
Costs - $535.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $37,192.45.

Additionally, the California Department of Motor Vehicles is enjoined from the transfer or sale of the vehicles described in paragraph 7 of the judgment without the written consent of plaintiff.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-15-2009 Continued To Nov-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

NOTICE OF APPLICATION OF WRIT OF POSSESSION AND HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Erin H. Welsch, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Pacific Service Credit Union

No other appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Pacific Service Credit Union's Application for writ of possession granted, provided that no writ shall issue until plaintiff has filed an undertaking in the sum of $9,000.00;
Redelivery bond is set at $9,200.00 for the 1999 Mercedes automobile, and $24,382.50 for the 2000 Porsche automobile, respectively. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
